reasoning: The most recent value for the series is 1.65 on 2025-10-24. In the past month, the series has fluctuated between 1.65 and 1.75, making 1.65 the local minimum of the recent trading range. Due to mean-reverting behavior of corporate bond spreads, there is a better-than-even chance that the value will bounce back above 1.65 in the near term. The linear trend estimate from FRED data suggests P(increase) = 0.72 for the first date, predicting a value around 1.678. I discount the "seasonal" estimate of 0.33 which simply reflects that absolute spread levels were lower in late 2024. Therefore, I place my short-term probability around 0.63 and shrink it towards 0.5 for larger forecast horizons, reflecting increased uncertainty.
